docs.text: Storage of OHBA imaging data Purpose Recommendation for storing of OHBA imaging data incluyding MEG, OPM and MRI data. We propose of use the Oxford Research File Service (RFS) for anonymised data (e.g. raw OPM data with no identiers). OneDrive can be used for non-anonymised data. Prerequisites A SSO account at University of Oxford Steps Requestions a RFS storage Follow steps on https://www.it.ox.ac.uk/article/oxford-research-file-service-rfs-available-for-storing-your-live-research-data (this will take you to the Service Catalogue where RFS can be requestes) Mounting RFS storage ensure you are on VPN Windows: In the File Explorer, click on This PC in the left hand panel. Press Map network drive, found in the ribbon. In the Folder text box, enter \connect.ox.ac.uk\ . should be mapped to the R: drive if possibl MAC-OS: In the Finder, select Go. Click Connect to server : Getting a key for link a participant data on OneDrive and RFS
